rulings.Case Title: DENNIS T. MANGANO VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 7, 2018, Line 1, RESPONDENT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION Respondent Regents of the University of California's demurrers are OVERRULED. Requirements for demurrers to civil complaints also apply in administrative mandamus. (See CCP §1109.) Among them: all the petition's allegations are deemed true and factual disputation is thus inappropriate. (See Kleiner v. Garrison (1947) 82 Cal.App.2d 442, 445.) As to the writ petition's first cause of action, respondent concedes that returning petitioner to his faculty position qualifies as a ministerial act (Memo. 8:1.), and then devolves into factual disputes. The petition also pleads that administrative remedies have been exhausted - an allegation again met with factual disputes. The materials of which judicial notice are taken are not to the contrary. The demurrers do not appear to challenge the petition's second cause of action. (See Rply. p. 3 n.2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ru

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. KINK.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cybernet Entertainment, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set Two)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 7, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set Two) Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant has the burden of overcoming Plaintiff's important and substantive privacy rights concerning his personal sexual history. Plaintiff has produced evidence of negative HIV tests on April 1, 2013 and April 30, 2013, and experts who have concluded, given the negative tests, the date of the onset of Plaintiff's symptoms, and the date of his first positive test result, that it is highly likely that the date of infection was between April 20, 2013 and May 12, 2013. Defendant, despite bearing the burden, has produced no affirmative evidence that a different window is more appropriate or that counters Plaintiff's evidence that the infection window is too narrow. Accordingly, Defendant has not met its burden to overcome Plaintiff's privacy rights and is only entitled to the information that Plaintiff has produced on his sexual contacts during the window of his likely infection. Therefore, Defendant's motion to compel is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Mint Collection -410-418 Jessie Street Condominium'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Subpoena For Production Of Documents To Veritas Investments Inc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 7, 2018, Line 3, Plaintiff Mint Collection -410-418 Jessie Street Condominium's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Subpoena For Production Of Documents To Veritas Investments Inc. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is Granted. Veritas Investments, Inc.'s objections are without merit. Plaintiff's requests appear designed to produce documents that are reasonably calculated to lead to information relevant to the claims in this litigation. Vertias' citation to CCP 1985.3 is inappropriate as these records do not appear to be covered by the statute. Veritas shall produce responsive documents to Plaintiff no later than September 17, 2018. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VIRAJ PATEL VS. BARRY EDWARD O'NEILL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue To Contra Costa County Or In The Alternative, Santa Clara
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 7, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTs BARRY O'NEILL and K.J. WOODS CONSTRUCTION, INC's Motion To Transfer Venue To Contra Costa County Or In The Alternative, Santa Clara Sixteen months after answering the complaint and two months before trial, defendants move to transfer venue under CCP §397(c). The motion is DENIED, first because it is much-belated. (See, e.g., Willingham v. Pecora (1941) 44 Cal.App.2d 289, 294-95; Thompson v. Sup. Ct. (1972) 26 Cal.App.3d 300, 309-10.) Second, witnesses are spread among five counties in the San Francisco Bay Area, where the San Francisco Superior Court is centrally and conveniently located. (See CCP §397(c).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ru

rulings.Case Title: FREZGI GEBREZGIABHER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 7, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANTS LUXOR CABS INCORPORATED, LUXOR EXECUTIVE CAR SERVICE, DANNY NGUYEN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Deposition is granted. Plaintiff's objections to the deposition notice on the grounds that discovery has closed are overruled and without merit. Plaintiff shall appear at his deposition at Defendants' offices, unless the parties stipulate in writing to another location. Plaintiff's deposition shall occur on September 17, 2018, unless the parties stipulate in writing to another date. Defendants shall provide and pay for a translator to assist Plaintiff in his deposition. Plaintiff and his counsel of record are sanctioned for misuse of the discover process. Plaintiff has not acted in good faith. Plaintiff agreed to continue discovery, then argued after the original discovery cut-off that discovery had closed. Plaintiff has agreed to set deposition dates, only to not produce Plaintiff for deposition. Plaintiff and his counsel are jointly and severally sanctioned in the amount of $5,175, payable to Defendants no later than October 8, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 7, 2018, Line 10, Plaintiff Devlin'S Motion For Summary Judgment On His Complaint For Breach Of The Settlement Agreement Plaintiff Devlin's motion for summary judgment on his complaint for breach of the settlement agreement is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: BING FU KUNG VS. ARMENTUM PARTNERS, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Excusing Plaintiff From Obtaining Defaults And Default Judgments Against Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 7, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF BING KUNG's Motion For An Order Excusing Plaintiff From Obtaining Defaults And Default Judgments Against Defendants The court treats plaintiff Kung's motion for order excusing plaintiff from obtaining defaults against defendants as a motion to stay and GRANTS the motion to stay. The instant derivative action is stayed until the direct action (Bing Fu Kung v. Christopher Carthy, et al. (CGC-18-565765) is resolved or further order of the court. Litigating the direct action will likely resolve most, if not all, of the issues in this derivative lawsuit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff kung is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ru
